title: Perfective Aspect Intro
description: Discover the difference between imperfective and perfective verb pairs (psát/napsat, číst/přečíst, dělat/udělat, kupovat/koupit). Learn how perfective verbs express completed actions in the past and how their present-tense forms actually express future meaning (napíšu = I will write). Practice using both aspects correctly.

exercises.prompt: Which verb is the perfective pair of 'psát' (to write)?

data.question_cz: Které sloveso je dokonavý (perfektivní) protějšek slovesa 'psát'?
data.question_en: Which verb is the perfective pair of 'psát' (to write)?
data.explanation: 'Napsat' is the basic perfective pair of 'psát'. The prefix 'na-' makes it perfective: psát (was writing) → napsat (wrote/has written). Other prefixed forms add meaning: popsat = to describe, přepsat = to rewrite, zapsat = to note down.

exercises.prompt: Complete with the perfective verb: 'I read the whole book.' (přečíst)

data.sentence: ___ jsem celou knihu. (přečíst — I read the whole book, completed action)
data.explanation: 'Přečetl jsem' = I read (and finished). 'Přečíst' is the perfective pair of 'číst'. Perfective expresses a completed action: 'přečetl jsem celou knihu' = I read the whole book (finished it). Imperfective 'četl jsem knihu' = I was reading a book (no completion).

exercises.prompt: Which sentence uses the perfective verb correctly?

data.question_cz: Která věta používá dokonavé sloveso správně?
data.question_en: Which sentence uses the perfective verb correctly?
options: Včera jsem psal dopis celý den.
data.explanation: 'Napsal jsem dopis.' = I wrote a letter (completed action, result achieved). Perfective 'napsat' focuses on the result. 'Psal jsem' would mean 'I was writing' (process, no completion info). Perfective is incompatible with 'každý den' (repeated) and 'rád' (liked to) — those need imperfective.

exercises.prompt: What does the perfective present-tense form 'napíšu' mean?

data.question_en: What does the perfective form 'napíšu' mean?
data.explanation: 'Napíšu' = I will write. This is a KEY rule: perfective verbs CANNOT express present tense! Their present-tense forms automatically express FUTURE meaning. So 'napíšu' (looks like present) = I'll write (future). 'Píšu' (imperfective) = I am writing (present).

exercises.prompt: Complete with the perfective future form: 'I'll read it tomorrow.' (přečíst)

data.sentence: Zítra to ___ . (přečíst — I'll read it, complete action)
data.explanation: 'Přečtu' is the perfective present form of 'přečíst' and it expresses future: 'Zítra to přečtu.' = I'll read it tomorrow (and finish it). Pattern: perfective present = future. Compare: 'čtu' (imperfective present) = I am reading, 'přečtu' (perfective present) = I will read.

exercises.prompt: What is the perfective pair of 'dělat' (to do/make)?

data.question_en: What is the perfective pair of 'dělat'?
data.explanation: 'Udělat' is the basic perfective pair of 'dělat'. The prefix 'u-' is one of the most common perfectivizers: dělat (was doing) → udělat (did/has done). 'Dodělat' = to finish making, 'předělat' = to redo, 'nadělat' = to make a lot of.

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'I (male) will do it today.' (Udělat — completed action in future)

data.source: I will do it today. (male speaker, completed action)
data.grammar_note: 'Udělám' is the perfective present form of 'udělat'. Since perfective verbs cannot express present, this form means 'I will do (and finish)'. 'Dělám to dnes' (imperfective) = I'm doing it today (may not finish).

exercises.prompt: Which verb pair shows the imperfective → perfective relationship for 'buy'?

data.question_cz: Která dvojice ukazuje vztah nedokonavé → dokonavé pro 'buy'?
data.question_en: Which pair shows imperfective → perfective for 'buy'?
data.explanation: 'Kupovat' (imperfective) → 'koupit' (perfective). Unlike most pairs that use a prefix, this pair uses a completely different verb stem (suppletion). 'Kupuju' = I'm buying (present), 'Koupím' = I'll buy (future, because perfective present = future).

exercises.prompt: Choose the correct aspect: 'I was buying bread when... (ongoing action)'

data.sentence: ___ jsem chleba, když jsem potkal kamaráda. (kupovat/koupit — I was buying bread when I met a friend)
data.explanation: Use imperfective 'kupoval' here because it describes an ongoing background action. The perfective 'koupil' would mean the buying was completed before meeting the friend. Typical pattern: imperfective (ongoing) + když + perfective (interruption).

exercises.prompt: Translate to Czech: 'She wrote the letter and sent it.' (both completed actions)

data.source: She wrote the letter and sent it.
accepted_answers: Ona napsala dopis a poslala ho.
data.grammar_note: Both actions are completed → both use perfective past: 'napsala' (napsat) and 'poslala' (poslat, perfective of posílat). Sequence of completed actions = perfective chain. 'Napsala dopis a poslala ho.' — each verb signals a finished step.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence means 'I will buy it tomorrow' (completed purchase)?

data.question_cz: Která věta znamená 'I will buy it tomorrow' (dokončený nákup)?
data.question_en: Which sentence means 'I will buy it tomorrow' (completed purchase)?
data.explanation: 'Koupím to zítra.' = I'll buy it tomorrow. 'Koupím' is the perfective present of 'koupit' → it expresses future. 'Kupuju' (imperfective present) cannot mean future. 'Budu kupovat' = I will be buying (imperfective future, ongoing process). Perfective is the natural choice for a one-time completed purchase.

exercises.prompt: Complete the conversation using both aspects correctly.

lines.speaker: person_b
lines.text: Psal jsem úkol. Ale už jsem ho napsal.

accepted_answers: Psal jsem úkol. Ale už jsem ho napsal.|Psal jsem úkol, ale už jsem ho napsal.|Psal jsem úkol. Už jsem ho napsal.
data.explanation: Person B uses both aspects: 'Psal jsem úkol' (imperfective — was writing, process) then 'už jsem ho napsal' (perfective — finished it). Then 'Přečtu ji zítra' (perfective present = future: I'll read it). Key pattern: process (impf.) → completion (pf.); future intention (pf. present).

exercises.answer_key: Psal jsem úkol. Ale už jsem ho napsal.
